update.currentPhase: Orion has completed its lunar flyby and is currently on a return trajectory toward Earth.
update.headline: Artemis II Orion spacecraft breaks human spaceflight distance record during lunar flyby.
details: Orion reached a maximum distance of 252,756 miles (406,771 km) from Earth, surpassing the Apollo 13 record.
The spacecraft completed its closest lunar approach at approximately 4,067 miles above the surface on Flight Day 6.
Crew radiation exposure is currently measured at 5.2 millisieverts, well within safe mission limits.
Orion is expected to exit the lunar sphere of influence on April 7 at 1:25 p.m. EDT.
update.crewStatus: Crew is performing well; experiencing expected minor shifts in heart rate and sleep patterns but maintaining baseline cognitive function.
update.lastEvent: Achievement of maximum distance from Earth at 7:07 p.m. EDT on April 6.
update.nextEvent: Exit from the lunar sphere of influence as Orion returns to Earth-dominant gravity.

milestones.label: Launch
milestones.detail: SLS liftoff from Pad 39B

milestones.label: Core Stage Separation
milestones.detail: MECO & core stage jettison

milestones.label: Solar Array Deploy
milestones.detail: Four Orion solar wings extended

milestones.label: Perigee Raise
milestones.detail: Orion performed perigee raise and apogee raise burns.

milestones.label: Proximity Ops
milestones.detail: Crew successfully demonstrated manual piloting of Orion near the ICPS upper stage.

milestones.label: Translunar Injection
milestones.detail: Six-minute Service Module engine burn successfully sent Orion toward the Moon.

milestones.label: ICPS Separation
milestones.detail: Upper stage jettisoned

milestones.label: AVATAR Scientific Payload Checkout
milestones.detail: Testing 'organ-on-a-chip' technology to study deep space radiation effects on bone marrow cells.

milestones.label: Earth Photography By Crew
milestones.detail: Crew captured images of Earth's auroras and zodiacal light.

milestones.label: Outbound Trajectory Correction (OTC-1)
milestones.detail: Trajectory correction burn was scrapped due to high precision of the TLI burn.

milestones.label: Hygiene Bay Inspection
milestones.detail: Non-critical smell from waste management system identified as a dormant heater unit.

milestones.label: Lunar Distance Equalization
milestones.detail: Orion officially became closer to the Moon than to Earth on Flight Day 4.

milestones.label: Thermal Protection Inspection
milestones.detail: External inspection confirmed heat shield tiles are in excellent condition.

milestones.label: Manual Pilot Demo
milestones.detail: Koch and Hansen tested Orion handling qualities in deep space for 41 minutes.

milestones.label: Urine System Sun-Soak
milestones.detail: Spacecraft maneuvered to warm the venting system and resolve a temperature-related blockage.

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: Orion officially surpassed the record set by the Apollo 13 crew in 1970.

milestones.label: Otc 3
milestones.detail: Refined Orion’s path for the lunar flyby with a 17.5-second burn.

milestones.label: Deep Space Cpr Demo
milestones.detail: Astronatus practiced emergency medical procedures in microgravity.

milestones.label: Archer Study Participation
milestones.detail: Crew monitoring physiological responses to deep-space radiation via wearable sensors.

milestones.label: Waste System Resolution
milestones.detail: Ground teams and crew successfully resolved intermittent issues with the onboard toilet system.

milestones.label: Urine Line Freeze
milestones.detail: A frozen line in the urine venting system was identified, triggering the use of contingency urinals.

milestones.label: Laser Comm Milestone
milestones.detail: Downlinked over 100GB of high-resolution data via laser-based communications system.

milestones.label: CSA Live Downlink
milestones.detail: Crew engaged in a live media event with students from across Canada.

milestones.label: Suit Systems Test
milestones.detail: Crew verified fit, pressurization, and mobility of OCSS suits in microgravity.

milestones.label: Orientale Basin Imaging
milestones.detail: Captured the first live close-up images of the Moon's western limb Orientale Basin since the Apollo era.

milestones.label: Lunar Sphere Of Influence
milestones.detail: Orion officially entered the Lunar Sphere of Influence where lunar gravity becomes the dominant force.

milestones.label: Female Distance Record
milestones.detail: Christina Koch officially sets the record for the farthest distance from Earth for a woman.

milestones.label: Lunar Crater Naming Ceremony
milestones.detail: The crew named a lunar crater 'Carroll' in honor of the Commander's late wife.

milestones.label: Lunar Surface Observation
milestones.detail: Began 7-hour observation period of 35 geological lunar features.

milestones.label: Lunar Flyby
milestones.detail: Spacecraft passed within 6,545 km of the lunar surface.

milestones.label: Human Distance Record
milestones.detail: New record for human distance from Earth set at 406,771 km.

milestones.label: Return Coast
milestones.detail: Spacecraft transitioned to an Earthbound free-return trajectory following lunar flyby.

milestones.label: Farthest Distance From Earth Record
milestones.detail: Reached a record distance of 252,756 miles from Earth, surpassing Apollo 13.

milestones.label: Earthrise Photo Ops
milestones.detail: Crew captured high-definition footage of Earth emerging from behind the Moon.

milestones.label: Communications Blackout
milestones.detail: Signal re-acquired after Orion emerged from the lunar far side.

milestones.label: Sans Medical Exams
milestones.detail: The crew is performing intraocular pressure monitoring to study spaceflight-associated vision changes.

milestones.label: Deep Space Solar Eclipse
milestones.detail: Crew conducted a 53-minute solar corona observation while the Sun was eclipsed by the Moon.

milestones.label: AVATAR Research Project
milestones.detail: Ongoing study of radiation and microgravity effects on organ-on-a-chip tissue analogs.

milestones.label: Radiation Shelter Demo
milestones.detail: Crew will construct a temporary 'storm shelter' inside Orion to test solar particle event protection.

milestones.label: ISS Crew Conversation
milestones.detail: Scheduled outreach event with crew members currently aboard the International Space Station.

milestones.label: Exit Lunar Sphere Of Influence
milestones.detail: Point at which Earth's gravity becomes the dominant force over lunar gravity.

milestones.label: Manual Piloting Demonstration #2
milestones.detail: Secondary test of Orion's handling characteristics in the return corridor.

milestones.label: Re-Entry
milestones.detail: Skip re-entry at ~5,000°F

milestones.label: Splashdown
milestones.detail: Revised target splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of San Diego.

history.currentPhase: Orion has completed its lunar flyby and is currently on a return trajectory toward Earth.
history.headline: Artemis II Orion spacecraft breaks human spaceflight distance record during lunar flyby.
details: Orion reached a maximum distance of 252,756 miles (406,771 km) from Earth, surpassing the Apollo 13 record.
The spacecraft completed its closest lunar approach at approximately 4,067 miles above the surface on Flight Day 6.
Crew radiation exposure is currently measured at 5.2 millisieverts, well within safe mission limits.
Orion is expected to exit the lunar sphere of influence on April 7 at 1:25 p.m. EDT.
history.crewStatus: Crew is performing well; experiencing expected minor shifts in heart rate and sleep patterns but maintaining baseline cognitive function.
history.lastEvent: Achievement of maximum distance from Earth at 7:07 p.m. EDT on April 6.
history.nextEvent: Exit from the lunar sphere of influence as Orion returns to Earth-dominant gravity.

history.currentPhase: Returning to Earth
history.headline: The Orion spacecraft is on a stable return trajectory after successfully completing a historic lunar flyby and setting a new human distance record.
details: Orion surpassed the Apollo 13 record by reaching a maximum distance of 252,756 miles from Earth.
The crew successfully conducted high-resolution imaging of the lunar farside and observed a 53-minute deep-space solar eclipse.
Astronauts are utilizing ARCHER wearable sensors to monitor physiological responses during the return transit.
The spacecraft remains on track for a Pacific Ocean splashdown on April 10, 2026.
history.crewStatus: The four-member crew is in excellent health and high spirits, continuing mission operations during the return coast.
history.lastEvent: Deep space solar eclipse observation behind the Moon.
history.nextEvent: Exit of Lunar Sphere of Influence

history.currentPhase: Earthbound Return Leg
history.headline: Orion spacecraft Integrity is Earthbound after breaking the all-time human distance record from Earth.
details: The crew reached a peak distance of 406,771 km from Earth, shattering the 56-year-old record held by Apollo 13.
Orion completed its closest lunar approach at a distance of 6,545 km from the surface.
Mission Control has officially transitioned the mission to the return phase as the spacecraft heads for a Friday splashdown.
Crew remains in excellent health with cumulative radiation doses well within safe operational limits at 5.2 mSv.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in high spirits and maintaining baseline health.
history.lastEvent: Re-acquisition of Earth communications and capture of Earthrise footage following lunar far-side flyby.
history.nextEvent: Transition out of the lunar sphere of influence scheduled for 17:25Z today.

history.currentPhase: Free-return trajectory return coast
history.headline: Artemis II crew breaks all-time human distance record and begins return coast to Earth.
details: Orion successfully reached a record-breaking distance of 406,773 km from Earth, surpassing the Apollo 13 record.
The crew completed a close lunar flyby at an altitude of 6,545 km and observed the lunar far side including the Orientale Basin.
A 40-minute communication blackout occurred as Orion passed behind the Moon, followed by a unique deep-space solar eclipse observation.
Crew remains in excellent health and is currently conducting radiation research via the AVATAR project tissue-analog study.
history.crewStatus: Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in excellent spirits; currently conducting geological observations and AVATAR biological research.
history.lastEvent: Deep-space total solar eclipse observation of the Sun passing behind the Moon
history.nextEvent: Trajectory correction maneuvers and radiation shielding storm shelter demonstration

history.currentPhase: Homeward-bound on free-return trajectory
history.headline: Orion completes historic lunar flyby and breaks Apollo 13 distance record as it begins the return journey.
details: Orion re-established communication at 23:25 UTC after a 40-minute blackout during its closest lunar approach.
The mission reached a record-breaking distance of 406,756 km from Earth, surpassing the Apollo 13 mark.
The crew is currently performing neuro-ocular exams and vision tests to monitor for SANS in deep space.
Orion's trajectory is now officially slung around the Moon for a direct return to Earth's atmosphere.
sources: NASA Mission Control Artemis II Update April 6, 2026
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman and crew are in excellent health and high spirits after observing the lunar far side.
history.lastEvent: Re-establishment of signal and Earthrise observation following lunar far side transit.
history.nextEvent: Crew observation of a solar eclipse as the Sun passes behind the Moon in one hour.

history.currentPhase: Lunar Flyby Transition
history.headline: Artemis II breaks all-time human spaceflight distance record as Orion approaches lunar far side.
details: Orion surpassed the Apollo 13 distance record at 17:56 UTC, reaching 400,171 km from Earth.
Spacecraft 'Integrity' entered the Lunar Sphere of Influence at 04:37 UTC and is accelerating toward the Moon.
A 40-minute communications blackout is scheduled to begin at 22:44 UTC as Orion passes behind the Moon.
Onboard waste management systems are fully operational after crew repairs; suit pressure tests were completed successfully.
history.crewStatus: Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits, having recently celebrated Easter in orbit and completed OCSS suit evaluations.
history.lastEvent: Broke the human spaceflight distance record of 400,171 km at 17:56 UTC.
history.nextEvent: 40-minute planned communications blackout (Loss of Signal) as Orion passes behind the Moon.

history.currentPhase: Lunar flyby and closest approach approach
history.headline: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record to become the most distant human spaceflight in history.
details: Orion officially surpassed the Apollo 13 record of 248,655 miles from Earth at 17:56 UTC today.
The crew is preparing for a 40-minute communication blackout as the spacecraft passes behind the far side of the Moon at 22:44 UTC.
Engineers successfully resolved an intermittent toilet system issue, restoring full functionality to the waste management system.
Science operations have commenced with observations of the Orientale Basin and historical Apollo landing sites.
history.crewStatus: Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in excellent health and recently celebrated a lunar Easter.
history.lastEvent: Surpassing the all-time human distance record from Earth at 17:56 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Communication blackout (Loss of Signal) during lunar far side passage.

history.currentPhase: Lunar Flyby
history.headline: Artemis II crew breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human distance from Earth during lunar flyby.
details: The crew officially surpassed the Apollo 13 record of 248,655 miles at 17:56 UTC.
Orion is currently performing a 7-hour lunar observation period to document 35 geological features.
The waste management system issues encountered on Day 2 have been fully resolved.
The spacecraft will reach its closest lunar approach of 6,550 km at 23:02 UTC.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and performing lunar observations.
history.lastEvent: Surpassed the Apollo 13 all-time human distance record from Earth at 17:56 UTC.
history.nextEvent: 40-minute Loss of Signal (LOS) during lunar far side passage starting at 22:44 UTC.

history.currentPhase: Lunar Flyby and Far Side Observation
history.headline: Artemis II crew officially breaks the all-time human distance record from Earth.
details: Orion surpassed the Apollo 13 record of 248,655 miles at 1:57 PM EDT and is continuing toward a peak distance of 252,760 miles.
Crew members are performing a seven-hour observation of the lunar far side, including photography of terrain not seen by humans in over 50 years.
A frozen urine venting line has required the use of backup Collapsible Contingency Urinals, though mission safety remains unaffected.
Orion will pass within 4,070 miles of the lunar surface at 7:02 PM EDT tonight during its closest approach.
sources: Artemis II Flight Operations Daily Log
history.alertMessage: A frozen urine venting line is currently being managed using backup systems; no risk to crew health or vehicle integrity identified.
history.crewStatus: Wiseman, Glover, Koch, and Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits; following microgravity fitness and emergency response protocols.
history.lastEvent: Transitioned into the Lunar Sphere of Influence at 12:37 AM EDT.
history.nextEvent: Closest approach to the lunar surface followed by a 40-minute total communication blackout as Orion passes behind the Moon.

history.currentPhase: Flight Day 6 - Lunar Observation Period
history.headline: Artemis II crew officially surpasses the all-time human distance record from Earth.
details: Orion surpassed the Apollo 13 record of 248,655 miles (400,171 km) at 17:56 UTC, officially becoming the farthest any humans have ever traveled.
The crew is currently in the lunar sphere of influence on a free-return trajectory approaching the Moon's far side.
A minor issue with the waste management system has been fully resolved, and all spacecraft systems are reporting nominal performance.
The crew conducted a ceremony naming a lunar crater 'Carroll' in memory of Commander Reid Wiseman's late wife.
history.crewStatus: Crew is in excellent health; they were woken up by a message from Jim Lovell and participated in a commemorative crater naming ceremony.
history.lastEvent: Surpassed the human distance record from Earth previously held by Apollo 13.
history.nextEvent: Loss of Signal (LOS) as Orion passes behind the far side of the Moon.

history.currentPhase: Lunar Slingshot and Record Distance Achievement
history.headline: Artemis II is minutes away from breaking the all-time human distance record during its lunar flyby phase.
details: Orion is set to surpass the Apollo 13 record of 248,655 miles from Earth at 17:56 UTC.
The crew is following a 'free-return trajectory' using lunar gravity to slingshot back toward Earth.
A technical issue with a frozen waste line in the Universal Waste Management System has been fully resolved.
Lunar observations of 35 specific surface targets, including Apollo landing sites, will begin at 18:45 UTC.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and high spirits.
history.lastEvent: Entered the Moon's sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC today.
history.nextEvent: Surpassing the Apollo 13 human distance record from Earth.

history.currentPhase: Orion has entered the lunar sphere of influence and is approaching its closest lunar flyby.
history.headline: Orion enters Moon's gravitational influence as crew prepares to shatter Apollo-era distance records.
details: Spacecraft entered the lunar sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC today, shifting gravitational dominance from Earth to the Moon.
The crew is approximately one hour away from surpassing the all-time human distance record of 248,655 miles set by Apollo 13.
A 40-minute communications blackout is expected at 22:44 UTC as Orion passes behind the lunar far side.
Commander Wiseman and his crew reported in excellent health following successful deep-space suit and medical response testing.
history.crewStatus: The four-person crew is in excellent health and high spirits; they recently received a morale-boosting message from Jim Lovell.
history.lastEvent: Entry into the lunar sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Surpassing the Apollo 13 record for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth.

history.currentPhase: Approaching Lunar Flyby and Maximum Distance
history.headline: Artemis II crew enters lunar sphere of influence as they prepare for record-breaking flyby.
details: Orion spacecraft 'Integrity' entered the lunar sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC today.
Crew is healthy and recently verified Orion Crew Survival System (OCSS) suit functionality.
A minor freeze in the urine venting line is being managed with backup collapsible contingency urinals.
The spacecraft is on a free-return trajectory following a 17.5-second correction burn.
history.crewStatus: The four-person crew is in excellent health and high spirits, observing the approaching lunar surface.
history.lastEvent: Outbound correction burn (OTC-3) completed at 03:03 UTC today to refine the lunar flyby path.
history.nextEvent: Breaking the Apollo 13 human distance record at 17:56 UTC.

history.currentPhase: Lunar Arrival / Outbound Flyby
history.headline: Orion enters Moon's sphere of influence ahead of record-breaking flyby.
details: Orion transitioned to lunar-dominant gravity at 04:37 UTC on Mission Day 6.
The crew has completed critical suit testing and cleared minor life support issues.
The spacecraft is on a precise free-return trajectory following the OTC-3 correction burn.
Orion is expected to surpass the all-time human distance record from Earth at 17:56 UTC today.
sources: NASA Artemis II Mission Control Update - April 6, 2026
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in excellent health and preparing for lunar observations.
history.lastEvent: Orion entered the lunar sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Surpassing the Apollo 13 record for farthest human distance from Earth at 17:56 UTC.

history.currentPhase: Lunar Flyby Approach
history.headline: Orion enters the Moon's gravitational sphere of influence as it prepares for record-breaking flyby.
details: Orion successfully reached the lunar sphere of influence at 04:41 UTC, with lunar gravity now the primary force on the craft.
A minor ice blockage in the urine vent was successfully cleared by ground controllers using remote line heaters.
The crew performed critical training including microgravity CPR and emergency donning of OCSS suits over the last 24 hours.
Spacecraft trajectory was refined via a 17.5-second outbound trajectory correction burn (OTC-3) late yesterday.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman and the crew are in excellent health and reported high spirits as they observed the lunar surface nearing.
history.lastEvent: Entry into the lunar sphere of influence at 04:41 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Surpassing the Apollo 13 all-time human distance record from Earth at 17:56 UTC.

history.currentPhase: Orion has entered the Moon's Sphere of Influence and is beginning its record-breaking lunar flyby approach.
history.headline: Artemis II enters lunar sphere of influence as crew prepares to break Apollo 13 distance record.
details: Orion entered the Moon's gravity-dominant sphere at 04:37 UTC today and is now accelerating toward the lunar surface.
The crew is in excellent health and recently completed a full checkout of the Orion Crew Survival System suits.
A waste management system issue involving a suspected ice blockage has been managed by flight controllers, and the system remains operational.
Within the next 12 hours, the mission is expected to surpass the 1970 record for the farthest humans have ever traveled from Earth.
history.crewStatus: Commander Wiseman, Pilot Glover, and Specialists Koch and Hansen are in high spirits and have cleared all medical response drills.
history.lastEvent: Orion entered the Lunar Sphere of Influence at 04:37 UTC on April 6.
history.nextEvent: Artemis II is expected to surpass the Apollo 13 human distance record from Earth at 17:56 UTC.

history.currentPhase: Approaching lunar flyby
history.headline: Orion spacecraft enters Moon's sphere of influence ahead of record-breaking lunar flyby.
details: Orion successfully entered the lunar sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC, currently positioned approximately 373,368 km from Earth.
The crew of four is in excellent health and nominal spirits, currently in their final sleep period before beginning historic flyby operations.
Mission milestones for today include surpassing the Apollo 13 distance record and achieving a closest lunar approach of 6,550 km.
Previously reported issues with the liquid waste system and external vent icing have been fully resolved by the crew.
sources: Aris Artemis II Flight Day 5 Status Report
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and performing nominal science and maintenance tasks.
history.lastEvent: Entry into the Moon's sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Surpassing the Apollo 13 distance record of 248,655 miles from Earth.

history.currentPhase: Lunar flyby and far side transit preparation
history.headline: Orion enters Moon's sphere of influence ahead of historic lunar flyby and record-breaking distance attempt.
details: Orion successfully entered the lunar sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC today, where lunar gravity is now the primary force acting on the craft.
Mission Specialist Christina Koch has officially become the farthest woman from Earth in human history.
A final 17.5-second outbound trajectory correction burn (OTC-3) was completed late last night to refine the approach to the lunar surface.
Initial technical issues with the Universal Waste Management System, including a frozen line, have been resolved by ground teams.
history.crewStatus: Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen are in excellent health and performing lunar science observations.
history.lastEvent: Successful entry into the lunar sphere of influence at 04:37 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Lunar flyby and communications blackout as Orion passes behind the Moon's far side.

history.currentPhase: Moon's sphere of influence entry and preparation for lunar flyby.
history.headline: Orion enters Moon's sphere of influence ahead of historic flyby and Apollo 13 distance record break.
details: Spacecraft successfully entered the Moon's sphere of influence at 04:41 UTC on Mission Day 5.
A 17.5-second outbound trajectory correction burn (OTC-3) was completed at 03:03 UTC to refine the flyby path.
Engineers are monitoring a non-critical malfunction in the wastewater tank venting system; cabin air quality remains safe.
The crew is currently in a scheduled sleep period, with wake-up planned for 14:50 UTC to begin Flight Day 6.
history.crewStatus: Crew is in excellent health and high spirits; currently resting ahead of the mission's closest lunar approach.
history.lastEvent: Orion entered the Moon's sphere of influence at 04:41 UTC on April 6, 2026.
history.nextEvent: Breaking the Apollo 13 human distance record at 17:56 UTC.

history.currentPhase: Orion is in the final hours of Flight Day 5, preparing for the lunar flyby after entering the Moon's sphere of influence.
history.headline: Orion enters lunar sphere of influence as crew prepares for record-breaking flyby.
details: Spacecraft successfully entered the Moon's sphere of influence at 04:41 UTC, making lunar gravity the dominant force.
Crew completed a 17.5-second Outbound Trajectory Correction burn (OTC-3) to refine the approach for the closest lunar pass.
Astronauts successfully conducted suit mobility and thermal tests in microgravity using the Orion Crew Survival System (OCSS).
Orion is on track to surpass the Apollo 13 distance record at 17:56 UTC today.
history.crewStatus: The crew is in excellent health and currently in a scheduled sleep period, set to wake at 14:50 UTC for Flight Day 6 activities.
history.lastEvent: Entry into the lunar sphere of influence at 04:41 UTC.
history.nextEvent: Surpassing the Apollo 13 distance record of 248,655 miles from Earth.
